sections.paragraph: f., a verbal noun of the conj. Hiphil, from the root זָכַר, in the signification of sacrificing, Isa 66:3 properly a memorial (offering), that which calls to memory. LXX., Vulg. μνημόσυνον, memoriale. This was the name of that part of the meat-offering [מִנְחָה] which was burned with frankincense upon the altar; the sweet savour of which ascending to heaven, was regarded as commending to God the remembrance of the worshipper. [But it must be borne in mind that this, as well as every other part of the law, was ordained by God himself.] Lev 2:2, , 16 5:12 Num 5:26. Lev 24:7, the frankincense also put on the loaves of shew-bread is called אַזְכָּרָה.
